column1: # Correspondence between the Rust trace-conformance checker's RICHER invariants
# (beyond the pure effect-status transition relation covered by
# effect-lifecycle-transitions.tsv) and the ControlPlaneLifecycle.tla invariants that
# This is the TLA half of the trace<->model bridge. It is enforced by
#     `richer_invariants_have_bite` builds, for each `key`, a trace that violates the
#     invariant and asserts check_trace REJECTS it with `violation_substring`. So each
#     richer invariant is exercised; dropping it from check_trace fails the test.
#   - TLA side: the script asserts each `tla_invariant` is present in
#     actually checks it). Removing it from the model fails the gate.
# A row can only be added/removed by changing all three in lockstep.
